L1.area: Sandbox runtime execution and expression evaluation engine

L2.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists due to a time-of-check time-of-use (TOCTOU) mismatch in how property access keys are validated. The sandbox validates keys by coercing them to strings during safety checks, but does not enforce that the same string value is used during the actual property lookup. An attacker can supply a specially crafted object whose string coercion returns different values on successive calls — returning a benign value during the security check and a sensitive value during the actual access. This allows bypassing prototype access restrictions and achieving arbitrary code execution outside the sandbox.

L3.area: Property access evaluation and key validation in the sandbox expression executor
L3.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists due to a time-of-check time-of-use (TOCTOU) mismatch in how property access keys are validated during expression evaluation. When the sandbox evaluates property accesses on objects, it checks the key against security policies but does not coerce non-string key objects to a stable string representation before both the check and the use. An attacker can supply an object with a custom string coercion method that returns different values on successive calls — returning a benign key during the security check and a protected key (such as a prototype chain property) during the actual property lookup. This enables bypassing prototype access restrictions and achieving arbitrary code execution outside the sandbox.

ground_truth.reason: A TOCTOU mismatch in property key validation allows attackers to pass objects with custom string coercion that return different values during the safety check versus the actual property access, enabling sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution.
